channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: About 4 in 10 worried about environmental impacts of AI: Survey
item.description: A new poll shows growing concerns among citizens over the potential environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence. The Associated Press-NORC Center poll, released Thursday, found about 4 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Can’t wait for the touchscreen MacBook Pro M6? This wild, affordable accessory brings touch to your current Mac
item.description: The LapTouch can add touchscreen functionality to any MacBook for a fraction of the cost of a new Mac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Blake, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fortnite leaks point to a The Simpsons-themed mini-season starting in November, and I’m genuinely worried for my wallet
item.description: Leaks point toward a The Simpsons crossover in Fortnite. A new key art image has been leaked, showing Homer Simpson and other skins.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: EU accuses Meta and TikTok of breaching transparency rules
item.description: The European Union says Meta and TikTok have breached transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mcneil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft has allegedly tasked Xbox to deliver a 30% profit margin, and if true, it could explain the reason behind its price hikes and mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox's job cuts and price increases could be due to a push from Microsoft to achieve a 30% profit margin.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Some of the top Sims 4 creators have just quit the game’s EA Creator Network, citing the ongoing EA sale to Saudi-backed investors – here’s what lilsimsie, James Turner and more have said in their statements
item.description: Here's what some of the top Sims 4 creators have to say on the proposed EA sale deal, including info on who's leaving the EA Creator Network.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: Qué medidas pueden tomar los CIO para progresar en la era de la IA
item.description: Si le pregunta a cualquier director de TI sobre la IA, probablemente le dirá que es ideal para realizar tareas repetitivas y de bajo nivel.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Enterprises should not install OpenAI’s new Atlas browser, analysts warn
item.description: Companies that might be eyeing OpenAI’s new ChatGPT Atlas browser should not rush to use it because of potential security risks, analysts said this week. The browser was unveiled on Tuesday after it had been teased for months as a work in progress. It is currently available for MacOS only.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI browsers can be abused by malicious AI sidebar extensions: Report
item.description: AI browsers may be smart, but they’re not smart enough to block a common threat: Malicious extensions. That’s the conclusion of researchers at SquareX, who on Thursday released a report showing how attackers can exploit AI sidebars through compromised browser extensions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens
item.description: Chris Ritter, the division director for scientific computing and AI at the Idaho National Lab, said using AI to improve basic workflow processes is step one. The post Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How Patmos set up an AI data center in 90 days
item.description: Data center hosting and compute services provider Patmos set up and deployed a new AI data center in just 90 days, when it usually takes two to three years to set up a colocation facility. All it needed was an unused printing press in the heart of Kansas City.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nissan adds solar roof to Japan's most popular little EV – and it promises to add 1,800 miles of free range a year
item.description: An extendable solar roof can add useable mileage to Nissan's diminutive EV and the tech is going into production.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scammers are targeting cloud systems to make off with hauls of gift cards
item.description: Gift cards are ideal for hackers since they can easily be redeemed or sold without sharing personally identifiable data.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump pardons Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, high-profile cryptocurrency figure
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, who created the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange and served prison time for failing to stop criminals from using the platform to move money connected to child sex abuse, drug trafficking and terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How AI can produce detailed storm surge forecasts faster and save lives
item.description: Hurricanes are America’s most destructive natural hazards, causing more deaths and property damage than any other type of disaster. Since 1980, these powerful tropical storms have done more than US$1.5 trillion in damage and killed more than 7,000 people. The No.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A practitioner’s primer on deterministic application modernization
item.description: Large organizations rarely have just a handful of applications. They have thousands, often representing billions of lines of code. These code bases span decades of frameworks, libraries, and shifting best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Police agencies turn to virtual reality to improve split-second decision-making
item.description: Police departments across the U.S. and Canada are adopting virtual reality training to better prepare officers for high-pressure, real-world situations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI chatbots are wrong about news 45% of the time, study finds
item.description: A new international study coordinated by the European Broadcasting Union (EBU) and led by the BBC shows that AI assistants distort news content nearly half of the time. The research included 22 public service broadcasters in 18 countries and 14 languages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to use Python dataclasses
item.description: Everything in Python is an object, or so the saying goes. If you want to create your own custom objects, with their own properties and methods, you use Python’s class object to do it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DCIM explained: How data center information management powers AI, capacity planning, and sustainability
item.description: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM) involves managing all aspects of the physical data center. DCIM converges IT management, operations management, facilities management, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI launches web browser ChatGPT Atlas: How to start using it
item.description: OpenAI announced Tuesday it is launching a ChatGPT-powered web browser called Atlas that will compete directly with widely used Google Chrome.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Google kills its cookie killer
item.description: Privacy Sandbox, Google’s attempt to create an alternative to cookies, looks like it has reached the end of the line. The company has announced that it is discontinuing 11 Privacy Sandbox technologies — pretty much the entire gamut.
item.dc:creator: Info World
